intro: Tauroceras cornigerum, also known as the horned Tauroceras, is a species of succulent plant in the family Aloeaceae. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including inflammation, wounds, and skin conditions, due to its antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and wound-healing properties. The bioactive constituents of Tauroceras cornigerum include anthraquinones, saponins, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal effects. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a herbal tea, juice, or ointment, and is used to treat various conditions, including eczema, acne, and dermatitis.
